title: Compile Quality Metrics v1 (Draft)
description: Schema for a Requirement Compiler quality metrics report. Captures 10 metrics (4 hard gates + 6 soft thresholds), gate verdicts per mode, and the data-source references used to compute each metric. Produced by the quality gate runner and consumed by validate.sh (gate integration spec only — not implemented in this sprint).

sprint_id.description: The sprint whose compiler output this report evaluates.

computed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this metrics report was produced.

mode.description: Gate enforcement mode. 'default': only 4 hard metrics block dispatch. 'strict-quality': all 10 metrics block dispatch (--strict-quality CLI flag).

gate_verdict.description: Overall gate verdict. 'pass': all hard gates (in current mode) met. 'fail': at least one hard gate not met. 'warn': all hard gates met but at least one soft threshold violated.

gate_failures.description: List of metric names that caused gate_verdict=fail. Empty if gate_verdict=pass or warn.

gate_warnings.description: List of metric names whose soft threshold was not met. Non-blocking. Empty if all soft metrics pass.

metrics.description: All 10 quality metrics. 4 hard gates (acceptance_coverage_rate, secret_leak_rate, evidence_ledger_completeness, replay_consistency_rate) block dispatch when failed. 6 soft metrics produce warnings only in default mode.

golden_set_pass_rate.description: Fraction of golden set cases that the compiler passes when replayed. Formula: passed_cases / total_cases. Data source: replay_result.json files from N2 replay suite run against golden-set-spec.md cases. Threshold: >= 0.95 (soft in default mode, hard in strict-quality mode).

field_coverage_rate.description: Fraction of required IR fields that are non-empty in compiled output. Formula: count(non_null_non_empty_required_fields) / count(total_required_fields) across all cases in window. Data source: IR schema validator output (requirement-ir.schema.v1). Threshold: >= 0.98 (soft in default mode, hard in strict-quality mode).

acceptance_coverage_rate.description: HARD GATE. Fraction of compiled acceptance[] items that have a non-empty mapped_to[] array. Formula: count(acceptance_items with mapped_to.length >= 1) / count(total_acceptance_items). Data source: grep on IR JSON files for mapped_to[] array contents. Threshold: = 1.00 (HARD in all modes — any unmapped acceptance item blocks dispatch).

secret_leak_rate.description: HARD GATE. Fraction of compiled IRs that contain a secret-like string after compilation. Formula: count(IRs with secret_detected=true) / count(total_compiled_IRs). Data source: secret scanner run over IR JSON output; uses pattern set from feedback-event.schema.v1 $comment + N3 secret-safe scrub patterns. Threshold: = 0.0 (HARD in all modes — any secret leak immediately blocks dispatch and triggers incident).

planner_diff_rate.description: Fraction of planner runs where the planner modified at least one IR-derived field in the compiled PRD. Formula: count(planner_runs with >= 1 planner_diff feedback-event) / count(total_planner_runs). Data source: planner_diff_log.jsonl (feedback-event.v1 with event_type=planner_diff). Threshold: <= 0.30 (soft in default mode, hard in strict-quality mode).

evaluator_reject_rate.description: Fraction of evaluator runs that produced at least one error-severity rejection reason. Formula: count(evaluator_runs with >= 1 rejection_reason.severity=error) / count(total_evaluator_runs). Data source: evaluator_reject_log.jsonl (feedback-event.v1 with event_type=evaluator_reject, severity=error). Threshold: <= 0.20 (soft in default mode, hard in strict-quality mode).

replay_consistency_rate.description: HARD GATE. Fraction of replay runs that produce byte-exact identical output when re-run on the same input. Formula: count(replays where output_hash_run1 == output_hash_run2) / count(total_replays). Data source: replay_result.json pairs (same case_id, two runs). Verifies deterministic compiler guarantee (C9). Threshold: >= 0.99 (HARD in all modes — non-deterministic compiler output is a critical correctness violation).

misclassification_rate.description: Fraction of compiled cases where the source/type classification is incorrect per human label. Formula: count(cases with human_label.class=misclassification) / count(total_human_labeled_cases). Data source: failure-replay archive human_label fields (failure_replay_entry.v1 human_label.class). Threshold: <= 0.05 (soft in default mode, hard in strict-quality mode).

miscompilation_rate.description: Fraction of compiled cases where field extraction or template substitution produced incorrect output per human label. Formula: count(cases with human_label.class=miscompilation) / count(total_human_labeled_cases). Data source: failure-replay archive human_label fields. Threshold: <= 0.10 (soft in default mode, hard in strict-quality mode).

evidence_ledger_completeness.description: HARD GATE. Fraction of research-type IRs that have a corresponding evidence ledger directory and at least one ledger entry. Formula: count(research-type IRs with evidence/ dir present AND ledger.jsonl non-empty) / count(total research-type IRs). Data source: filesystem check `test -d sprints/<sid>.evidence/ && wc -l sprints/<sid>.evidence/ledger.jsonl`. Threshold: = 1.00 (HARD in all modes — research IR without evidence ledger is a gate violation per C8).

value.description: Computed metric value in [0.0, 1.0]. null if metric could not be computed (e.g., no data in window).

strict_quality.description: Threshold value in --strict-quality mode. Must equal default for hard gates (same threshold regardless of mode).

comparison.description: Comparison operator: gte=>=, lte=<=, eq===. Applied as: value <comparison> threshold.

gate_kind.description: hard: metric blocks dispatch in default mode AND strict-quality mode when threshold is not met. soft: metric only warns in default mode; blocks in strict-quality mode.

passed.description: Whether the threshold was met under the current mode. null if value is null (metric uncomputed).

numerator.description: Raw numerator of the metric formula. Optional — for auditability.

denominator.description: Raw denominator of the metric formula. null if metric could not be computed. Optional — for auditability.

data_source_refs.description: List of file paths or system references where the metric data was collected from. At least one required for auditability.

note.description: Optional human-readable note about this metric computation (e.g., 'computed over last 30 days', 'no research-type cases this sprint — trivially 1.00').
